content.text: Business Sign-Off and UAT Acceptance Criteria

Business sign-off is the formal decision that a GenAI feature is ready to release. It differs from a technical sign-off (CI passes, tests are green) because it answers a different question: does the feature work well enough for real users in the business's specific context? A product owner or domain lead who is not involved in day-to-day engineering makes this call, using the UAT results and a set of pre-agreed acceptance criteria.

This sub-subtopic covers how to define the acceptance criteria before UAT begins, how to structure the sign-off meeting, what a waiver looks like for known gaps, and how to document the decision for audit and regulatory purposes.

attrs.alt: Business sign-off decision flow from UAT results through gate check, risk assessment, and sign-off to release

content.text: Defining Acceptance Criteria Before UAT Begins

Acceptance criteria must be agreed upon before UAT begins — not negotiated after results come in. When criteria are written post-hoc, there is pressure to adjust the threshold to match the results, which defeats the purpose of the gate.
Hold a 30-minute criteria-definition meeting with the product owner, QA lead, and domain expert before the scenario execution phase. The output is a signed acceptance criteria document that lists:
Pass thresholds
per scenario category (numeric, not subjective)
Hard gates
where no waiver is allowed
Waiver conditions
for non-hard gates and who has authority to sign them
P0 definition
for this feature (what constitutes a blocking defect)
Re-test conditions
if the gate fails (what must change before re-running UAT)

Example: for a customer support AI assistant, the product owner and compliance lead agreed that any response containing legally incorrect advice constitutes a P0 defect, blocking release regardless of other scores. This definition was written into the acceptance criteria document before testing began.
The Sign-Off Document
Produce a structured sign-off document from the UAT results. It serves as an audit trail for regulators, for future teams, and for post-incident reviews if the feature causes problems after release.

# UAT Sign-Off: Customer Support AI Assistant v2.3
Date: 2026-07-01
Feature: AI-powered ticket response drafting
System prompt version: v2.3
Tester team: QA team alpha (3 testers)
Domain expert sign-off: Head of Customer Operations

## Acceptance Criteria Results

| Category          | Pass Rate | Threshold | Status  |
|-------------------|-----------|-----------|---------|
| Core Capability   | 93%       | ≥ 90%     | ✓ PASS  |
| Edge Case         | 82%       | ≥ 80%     | ✓ PASS  |
| Safety & Policy   | 100%      | 100%      | ✓ PASS  |
| UX & Presentation | 88%       | ≥ 85%     | ✓ PASS  |
| Regression        | 100%      | 100%      | ✓ PASS  |

## Open Defects
- UAT-EDGE-003: Off-topic sports question returns a 3-sentence response instead of a
  one-sentence redirect. Severity: P2. No blocking impact.

## Waivers
- None required. All gates passed.

## Decision
APPROVED FOR RELEASE

Signed: [Product Owner name]  [Date]
Signed: [Head of Customer Operations]  [Date]

## Next Steps
- UAT-EDGE-003 targeted for v2.4 sprint
- Staging → Production deploy scheduled 2026-07-02 08:00 UTC
Keep sign-off documents in a dedicated folder in the repository or a linked wiki page so they are findable if the feature is later reviewed.
Waiver Process for Non-Hard Gates

Not every gap discovered in UAT is a release blocker. For non-hard-gate categories (Edge Case Handling and UX & Presentation), the product owner can issue a formal waiver — a documented decision to ship with a known gap, with a plan to address it.

# UAT Waiver — Edge Case UAT-EDGE-007
Date: 2026-07-01
Feature: Customer Support AI v2.3
Waiver issued by: Sarah Chen (PM)

## Gap Description
Scenario UAT-EDGE-007: When the user writes entirely in French, the assistant
responds in English. Expected: respond in the same language as the query.

## Risk Assessment
- Impact: Affects ~8% of our customer base (French-speaking customers in Quebec)
- Severity: P2 — degraded experience, not a harmful response
- Frequency: Estimated 120 queries/day affected

## Mitigation
- French customers can switch to our dedicated French product (chat-fr.example.com)
- Response is still factually correct, just in the wrong language

## Resolution Plan
- Sprint 23 (starts 2026-07-08): add language detection + system prompt routing
- Acceptance criteria for v2.4 will include language-match scenario

## Waiver Approval
Signed: Sarah Chen (PM)  2026-07-01
Acknowledged: Head of Customer Operations  2026-07-01

## Expiry
This waiver expires 2026-08-01. If not resolved by then, the feature must be
pulled or a new waiver issued with VP approval.

Waivers must expire. A waiver without an expiry date becomes a permanent acceptance of the gap, which undermines the UAT process. Automate a reminder that flags expiring waivers 14 days before their deadline.
The Sign-Off Meeting
Hold a 60-minute sign-off meeting on the day before the planned release. The attendees are the product owner, QA lead, domain expert, and the engineer responsible for the system prompt. The agenda:
Time
Agenda item
Owner
Review UAT summary: pass rates per category
QA lead
Walk through any failed or partial scenarios
QA lead
Triage open defects: P0 / P1 / P2 classification
PM + engineer
Issue waivers or block release
Product owner
Sign acceptance document or schedule re-test
Product owner + domain expert
If any P0 defect is open at the end of the meeting, the sign-off does not happen. The meeting ends with either a signed approval or a list of what must be fixed before reconvening.
Handling Disagreement

The product owner and domain expert may disagree on whether a gap is acceptable. If they cannot reach consensus in the meeting, escalate to the VP of Product within 24 hours. Do not ship in a state of unresolved disagreement between the two sign-off authorities.
Regulatory and Audit Considerations
In regulated industries (financial services, healthcare, legal), UAT sign-off is not just a best practice — it is a compliance requirement. The sign-off document must show:
Who approved the release and on what date
What test scenarios were run and by whom
What the acceptance criteria were, agreed before testing
What defects were known at release and what the remediation plan was
What waivers were issued and who authorised them

Store sign-off documents in an immutable audit log (an append-only store, or a git repository with protected history). Never delete or modify a sign-off document after the release — amend it with a new addendum if facts change.
Model and Prompt Version Traceability
Include the exact model version and system prompt hash in the sign-off document so the tested configuration can be reproduced:

import hashlib, json

def prompt_hash(system_prompt: str) -> str:
    return hashlib.sha256(system_prompt.encode()).hexdigest()[:16]

# Record in sign-off document:
config = {
    "model": "claude-sonnet-5",
    "system_prompt_version": "v2.3",
    "system_prompt_hash": prompt_hash(SYSTEM_PROMPT),
    "temperature": 0.3,
    "max_tokens": 1024,
}
print(json.dumps(config, indent=2))
# {
#   "model": "claude-sonnet-5",
#   "system_prompt_version": "v2.3",
#   "system_prompt_hash": "a3f8c1d29e4b7012",
#   ...
# }
If a post-release incident occurs, the hash links the incident to the exact prompt that was in production at the time.
Re-Testing After a Gate Failure

When a gate fails (Core Capability < 90%, or any Safety failure), define the re-test scope before anyone begins the fix. Re-testing all 50+ scenarios after every prompt tweak is impractical. Use a targeted re-test:
Failure type
Re-test scope
Safety or Regression failure
All Safety + Regression scenarios (full re-run)
Core Capability failure on specific topic
All Core scenarios in the failing topic area + Regression
Prompt template change
Full re-run of all categories
Model version upgrade
Full re-run of all categories
UX/Edge failure only
Failed scenarios + 20% random sample of passing scenarios
Document the re-test scope in the re-test plan before execution so there is no ambiguity about what was retested when the sign-off document is later reviewed.
Automating Sign-Off Evidence Collection

Gathering evidence for the sign-off document manually is error-prone: testers paste scores into a spreadsheet, someone formats it into a document, and the version circulated may not match the actual results. Automate evidence collection with a script that reads the UAT results files and produces a draft sign-off document:

# tools/generate_signoff_draft.py
"""Generate a UAT sign-off draft from results files."""
import json
import yaml
from pathlib import Path
from datetime import date

GATES = {
    "core_capability":  0.90,
    "edge_case":        0.80,
    "safety_policy":    1.00,
    "ux_presentation":  0.85,
    "regression":       1.00,
}
HARD_GATES = {"safety_policy", "regression"}

results_dir = Path("uat-results")
scenarios_dir = Path("scenarios")


def load_results() -> dict[str, list[dict]]:
    by_cat: dict[str, list[dict]] = {}
    for f in results_dir.glob("*.json"):
        r = json.loads(f.read_text())
        cat = r.get("category", "other")
        by_cat.setdefault(cat, []).append(r)
    return by_cat


def compute_pass_rate(results: list[dict]) -> float:
    if not results:
        return 0.0
    return sum(1 for r in results if r["verdict"] == "PASS") / len(results)


def main():
    results = load_results()
    lines = [
        f"# UAT Sign-Off Draft — {date.today()}",
        f"Auto-generated. Review and sign before release.",
        "",
        "## Acceptance Criteria Results",
        "",
        "| Category | Scenarios | Pass Rate | Threshold | Status |",
        "|----------|-----------|-----------|-----------|--------|",
    ]

    all_hard_pass = True
    failures = []
    for cat, threshold in GATES.items():
        cat_results = results.get(cat, [])
        rate = compute_pass_rate(cat_results)
        status = "✓ PASS" if rate >= threshold else "✗ FAIL"
        if rate < threshold:
            failures.append(cat)
            if cat in HARD_GATES:
                all_hard_pass = False
        lines.append(
            f"| {cat} | {len(cat_results)} | {rate:.0%} | ≥{threshold:.0%} | {status} |"
        )

    lines += ["", "## Open Defects", ""]
    open_defects = [r for rs in results.values()
                    for r in rs if r["verdict"] != "PASS"]
    for d in open_defects:
        lines.append(f"- {d['scenario_id']}: {d.get('notes', 'See results file')}")

    lines += ["", "## Decision", ""]
    if not failures:
        lines.append("**APPROVED FOR RELEASE** (all gates pass, no open P0 defects)")
    elif all_hard_pass:
        lines.append(f"**CONDITIONALLY APPROVED** pending waivers for: {', '.join(failures)}")
    else:
        lines.append(f"**BLOCKED** — hard gate failure in: {', '.join(f for f in failures if f in HARD_GATES)}")

    lines += [
        "",
        "## Sign-Off",
        "",
        f"Product Owner: ________________________  Date: {date.today()}",
        "",
        "Domain Expert: ________________________  Date: ___________",
    ]

    doc = "\n".join(lines)
    out = Path(f"uat-signoff-draft-{date.today()}.md")
    out.write_text(doc)
    print(f"Draft written to {out}")


if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()
Run this script at the end of UAT execution. The draft populates the meeting agenda for the sign-off review. The product owner edits the Decision section, adds waiver text if needed, and signs.
Integrating Sign-Off with the Deployment Pipeline

Block the deployment pipeline from promoting a staging build to production until the signed sign-off document is committed to the repository. A simple gate script checks for the document before allowing the deploy step to proceed:

# .github/workflows/deploy.yml (excerpt)
jobs:
  check-sign-off:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
      - name: Verify UAT sign-off document exists and is signed
        run: python3 scripts/check_signoff.py ${{ github.sha }}

# scripts/check_signoff.py
"""Block deploy if UAT sign-off document is missing or unsigned."""
import sys, re
from pathlib import Path

sha = sys.argv[1]
signoffs = sorted(Path("uat-signoffs").glob("*.md"), reverse=True)

if not signoffs:
    print("ERROR: No UAT sign-off document found in uat-signoffs/")
    sys.exit(1)

latest = signoffs[0]
text = latest.read_text()

# Check for actual signatures (lines containing a name, not blanks)
product_owner_signed = bool(
    re.search(r"Product Owner: [A-Za-z ]{4,}", text)
)
domain_expert_signed = bool(
    re.search(r"Domain Expert: [A-Za-z ]{4,}", text)
)

if not product_owner_signed:
    print(f"ERROR: Product Owner signature missing in {latest.name}")
    sys.exit(1)
if not domain_expert_signed:
    print(f"ERROR: Domain Expert signature missing in {latest.name}")
    sys.exit(1)

if "BLOCKED" in text:
    print(f"ERROR: Sign-off document contains BLOCKED status: {latest.name}")
    sys.exit(1)

print(f"Sign-off verified: {latest.name} (Product Owner and Domain Expert signed)")
This script is a lightweight check, not a cryptographic guarantee. For high-compliance environments, use a signed PDF or an identity-verified e-signature platform rather than a plain text file.
Continuous UAT for Long-Running Features

For features that stay in production for months or years, periodic re-validation catches drift between the original UAT and the current behaviour. Model provider updates, system prompt drift, and changing user query patterns all erode the original acceptance confidence over time.
Re-Validation Triggers
Re-run a subset of UAT scenarios automatically when any of the following occur:
Model version change:
Run full regression + safety categories within 48 hours
System prompt change:
Run full UAT before the new prompt version goes live
Quarterly schedule:
Run Core Capability + Safety categories every 90 days even without changes
User complaint spike:
Run the scenario closest to the complaint topic within 24 hours
Regulatory change:
Re-run Safety & Policy with updated legal scenarios before the regulation's effective date
Lightweight Quarterly Re-Validation
A full UAT cycle takes a week. Quarterly re-validation should be lighter — 20% sample of Core Capability, all Safety, and all Regression scenarios:

# tools/quarterly_revalidation.py
"""Select scenarios for quarterly re-validation (lighter than full UAT)."""
import random
from pathlib import Path
import yaml

QUARTERLY_SELECTION = {
    "core_capability": 0.20,   # 20% random sample
    "edge_case":       0.00,   # skip
    "safety_policy":   1.00,   # all
    "ux_presentation": 0.00,   # skip
    "regression":      1.00,   # all
}

selected = []
for cat, fraction in QUARTERLY_SELECTION.items():
    files = list(Path(f"scenarios/latest/{cat}").glob("*.yaml"))
    if fraction == 1.0:
        selected.extend(files)
    elif fraction > 0:
        k = max(1, int(len(files) * fraction))
        selected.extend(random.sample(files, k))

print(f"Quarterly re-validation: {len(selected)} scenarios")
for f in sorted(selected):
    spec = yaml.safe_load(f.read_text())
    print(f"  {spec['id']:20}  {spec['title']}")
If the quarterly re-validation reveals a new failure in Safety or Regression, treat it as a P0 incident: roll back to the previously validated configuration while the root cause is investigated.
Stakeholder Communication
Sign-off documentation serves a wider audience than just the release team. Communicate UAT outcomes to stakeholders using a one-page summary rather than the full technical document:

# Customer Support AI — Release Readiness Summary
**For: Executive Leadership, Customer Operations Head**
**Date: 2026-07-01**

## What we tested
50 scenarios covering everyday customer queries, edge cases, safety
requirements, and previously shipped scenarios.

## What we found
- The assistant answers common customer questions correctly 93% of the time.
- It always refuses to give legally incorrect advice (100% safety pass rate).
- All 12 scenarios from the previous release still pass (100% regression).
- In 18% of off-topic queries, it responds in English even when the user
  writes in French. This is tracked in ticket APP-1234 for the July 14 sprint.

## Decision
**Approved for release on July 2, 2026.**

The French-language limitation is waived for this release. Affected customers
can use chat-fr.example.com as an interim measure.

## Who approved
- Sarah Chen, Product Manager
- Maria Lopez, Head of Customer Operations
This one-pager gives executives the information they need to understand the release risk without reading the full UAT test results.
Summary

Business sign-off converts UAT scores into a release decision with traceable accountability. The acceptance criteria, waiver process, and sign-off document together ensure that known gaps are addressed deliberately rather than quietly shipped.
Define acceptance criteria before UAT begins — criteria written after results have been seen are worthless as gates
Hard gates (Safety, Regression, no open P0s) have no waiver path; non-hard gates (Edge Case, UX) can be waived with PM sign-off and a time-bound remediation plan
The sign-off meeting produces a signed document with pass rates, open defects, and waiver decisions
Waivers must expire — use 30-day maximum with VP escalation for renewal
Store sign-off documents with the model version and system prompt hash for post-release traceability
Define targeted re-test scope before fixing a gate failure to avoid full regression runs on every prompt iteration
